annotations.label: Spherical harmonics

anchor.snippet: spherical harmonics are special functions defined on the surface of a sphere

annotations.note: No definition of spherical harmonics exists in Mathlib (no SphericalHarmonic, sphericalHarmonic, or related symbols found).

annotations.label: Harmonic functions

anchor.snippet: Functions that are solutions to Laplace's equation are called harmonics

annotations.note: Harmonic functions on real finite-dimensional inner product spaces are defined as twice continuously differentiable with vanishing Laplacian.

annotations.label: Laplace's spherical harmonics

anchor.snippet: are known as Laplace's spherical harmonics, as they were first introduced by Pierre Simon de Laplace in 1782

annotations.note: No specific definition of Laplace's spherical harmonics is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Completeness relation

anchor.snippet: The completeness relation is a distributional equality between a sum over all indices and the Dirac delta function

annotations.note: Mathlib has Parseval for Fourier series on the circle but not for spherical harmonics.
